public_release_boundary.description: Representative reproducibility subset with a documented reconstruction pathway. The subset alone is not a claim of complete empirical reproducibility for the full study.
public_release_boundary.licensing_notice: This representative subset is provided for reproducibility demonstration. Users should consult original upstream sources for full source licensing.

full_dataset.availability: Not shipped in the public release. Local build source path only.

states.note: All states present in the full final table appear in the released subset.

state_season_groups.note: State-season coverage is complete even though district and month coverage are partial.

regions.note: Region coverage is intentionally partial. The subset is a demo artifact, not a national district release.

months.note: Month coverage is intentionally partial and should not be described as a complete temporal reconstruction.

dominant_crops.note: Counts crops that appear as the row-wise top target at least once.

positive_target_columns.note: Counts target columns that are positive at least once, regardless of whether they are ever dominant.

quality_variation.note: Observed quality variation in the released subset is limited. Rows include both soil-imputed and non-imputed cases, but do not include positive time_step_missing or climate_gap_filled examples.

scope_decision: No extra anchor rows were added for release v1.0.0-public-artifact. The existing 211-row subset already covers all states, all state-season groups, all dominant crops, and all target columns while remaining explicitly partial for regions and months.

selection_strategy.state_season_extremes: Per state and season, include lowest- and highest-rainfall rows with quality-aware tie breaks.
selection_strategy.crop_exemplars: For every crop_prob_* target, include the row with the maximum observed probability.
selection_strategy.quality_exemplars: For every state, include the lowest-confidence row to preserve imputation and quality variation.
selection_strategy.deduplication: Union the selected rows and remove duplicates deterministically.

columns.name: region
columns.description: Human-readable district or region name used in the final ML-ready table.
columns.source: Merged district-level records after name canonicalization.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Canonicalized district labels aligned across climate, soil, and crop inputs.

columns.name: state
columns.description: State name associated with the district row.
columns.source: Merged district-level records after name canonicalization.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Used as fallback grouping key during imputation and for slice analysis.

columns.name: region_key
columns.description: Stable internal district identifier combining normalized state and district tokens.
columns.source: Derived key from canonicalized district and state names.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Primary join key across prepared climate, soil, and crop tables.

columns.name: time
columns.description: Monthly district-time key in YYYY-MM format.
columns.source: Derived temporal index after alignment to monthly granularity.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Climate and crop sources are aligned to the same monthly period key.

columns.name: temp_avg
columns.description: Average temperature for the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from weather data described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Aggregated from daily observations to a monthly mean.

columns.name: rain_total
columns.description: Total rainfall accumulated over the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from district rainfall data.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Aggregated from daily rainfall observations to a monthly sum.

columns.name: humidity_avg
columns.description: Average relative humidity for the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from weather data described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Aggregated from daily humidity observations to a monthly mean.

columns.name: rain_variance
columns.description: Variance of daily rainfall within the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from district rainfall data.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Computed from daily rainfall after temporal aggregation.

columns.name: max_temp
columns.description: Maximum daily temperature observed within the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from weather data described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Monthly maximum over daily maximum temperature values.

columns.name: max_temp_3d
columns.description: Peak 3-step rolling average of maximum temperature within the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from weather data described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Computed as the maximum rolling mean over the daily max temperature series.

columns.name: max_rain_1d
columns.description: Maximum single-day rainfall observed within the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from district rainfall data.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Monthly maximum over daily rainfall totals.

columns.name: dry_spell_days
columns.description: Longest run of low-rainfall days within the district-month.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from district rainfall data.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Counts the longest consecutive period with rainfall at or below the configured dry-spell threshold.

columns.name: temp_lag_7
columns.description: Coarse lag proxy for recent temperature conditions.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from weather data described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: After monthly aggregation, the previous period temperature is reused as a compatibility proxy for a shorter lag.

columns.name: rain_lag_14
columns.description: Coarse lag proxy for recent rainfall conditions.
columns.source: Prepared climate table built from district rainfall data.
columns.preprocessing_notes: After monthly aggregation, the previous period rainfall total is reused as a compatibility proxy for a shorter lag.

columns.name: pH
columns.description: Soil acidity or alkalinity indicator for the district.
columns.source: Prepared soil table derived from soil property sources described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Region-level mean after spatial alignment and numeric imputation.

columns.name: N
columns.description: District-level nitrogen proxy used by the model.
columns.source: Prepared soil table derived from soil property sources described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: The internal prep report states these N values are proxy estimates rather than direct observed lab N measurements.

columns.name: P
columns.description: District-level phosphorus proxy used by the model.
columns.source: Prepared soil table derived from soil property sources described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: The internal prep report states these P values are proxy estimates rather than direct observed lab P measurements.

columns.name: K
columns.description: District-level potassium proxy used by the model.
columns.source: Prepared soil table derived from soil property sources described in project references.
columns.preprocessing_notes: The internal prep report states these K values are proxy estimates rather than direct observed lab K measurements.

columns.name: N_class
columns.description: Categorical nitrogen status bucket.
columns.source: Derived from N using configured nutrient bins.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Binned into low, medium, or high categories before modeling.

columns.name: P_class
columns.description: Categorical phosphorus status bucket.
columns.source: Derived from P using configured nutrient bins.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Binned into low, medium, or high categories before modeling.

columns.name: K_class
columns.description: Categorical potassium status bucket.
columns.source: Derived from K using configured nutrient bins.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Binned into low, medium, or high categories before modeling.

columns.name: soil_health_index
columns.description: Simple composite soil support score used in the ML table.
columns.source: Derived from pH and N/P/K proxies.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Code comments explicitly describe this as a simple composite baseline, not an official scientific soil health card metric.

columns.name: irrigation_index
columns.description: Bounded management-context feature representing relative irrigation support.
columns.source: Context defaults plus heuristic inference from climate and soil conditions.
columns.preprocessing_notes: When not available from context defaults, inferred heuristically from rainfall, dry spells, humidity, heat, and soil support signals.

columns.name: rotation_score
columns.description: Bounded management-context feature representing crop-rotation suitability.
columns.source: Context defaults plus heuristic inference from soil and climate conditions.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Heuristic score built from soil health, nutrient balance, pH balance, and humidity when no direct value is available.

columns.name: fertility_class
columns.description: Categorical fertility summary token used in modeling and UI defaults.
columns.source: Context defaults and derived soil support context.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Filled from configured defaults when not explicitly available.

columns.name: state_context
columns.description: Normalized state token retained as a model context feature.
columns.source: Derived from state.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Lowercased string field used as categorical context during training and inference.

columns.name: region_context
columns.description: Normalized region token retained as a model context feature.
columns.source: Derived from region_key.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Lowercased stable region identifier used as categorical context during training and inference.

columns.name: target_month
columns.description: Zero-padded month token extracted from the district-time key.
columns.source: Derived from the aligned time index.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Stored in the released CSV text as a zero-padded string token from 01 to 12.

columns.name: target_season
columns.description: Season label derived from the target month.
columns.source: Derived from the aligned time index.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Mapped to kharif, rabi, or zaid using the project season lookup.

columns.name: time_step_missing
columns.description: Indicator for rows introduced to complete missing district-time periods.
columns.source: Dataset quality metadata.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Set during time-grid completion before climate imputation.

columns.name: climate_gap_filled
columns.description: Indicator that climate values were filled or imputed for the row.
columns.source: Dataset quality metadata.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Tracks climate gaps originating in raw series or introduced during merge alignment.

columns.name: soil_imputed
columns.description: Indicator that at least one soil field required imputation.
columns.source: Dataset quality metadata.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Triggered when soil values are missing and filled from district, state, or global summaries.

columns.name: geo_confidence
columns.description: Confidence score for the geographic alignment used during source joins.
columns.source: Geographic canonicalization and merge metadata.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Average of available geo-confidence signals from prepared source tables.

columns.name: data_confidence
columns.description: Composite quality score summarizing interpolation and imputation burden for the row.
columns.source: Dataset quality metadata.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Computed from climate interpolation, soil imputation, missing time-step, and merge-imputation penalties.

columns.name: crop_prob_arcanut_processed
columns.description: Normalized district-time probability target for arcanut processed.
columns.source: Prepared crop table derived from historical area or production records.
columns.preprocessing_notes: Aggregated by district, month, and crop, then row-normalized so all crop_prob_* targets sum to 1.
